question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user register an account such that its `deleted` column is set to NULL rather than false, causing `deleted <> true` to evaluate to NULL (not TRUE) in SQL, making `findByAlias` return empty and permanently blocking alias-based fund transfers for that account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SQL three-valued logic gap in `deleted <> true` (which silently excludes NULL-deleted entities) to cause a victim account's alias lookup to return empty, effectively freezing all alias-routed fund transfers without any freeze transaction being issued?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a state where an account's `deleted` field transitions to NULL during an update race condition, causing `findByAlias` to silently drop the entity from results and permanently block all alias-based fund movements for that account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `deleted <> true` condition in `findByEvmAddress` to cause an account with `deleted = NULL` to be invisible to EVM address lookups, permanently freezing all EVM-address-routed funds for that account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user craft an alias byte array of unexpected length (e.g., empty array, single byte, or oversized blob) that causes `findByAlias` to match an unintended entity row, leading to freeze operations being applied to the wrong account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user supply an EVM address byte array shorter or longer than 20 bytes to `findByEvmAddress`, causing PostgreSQL's bytea comparison to match an unintended entity and routing freeze operations to a victim account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getSupply(String, String)] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the `lowerBounds` or `upperBounds` string parameters passed to `getSupply` to include entity IDs of frozen accounts in the supply calculation, causing the system to misreport available supply and triggering downstream fund-locking logic?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getSupply(String, String)] Can an unprivileged external user inject a malformed comma-separated string into `lowerBounds` or `upperBounds` that causes `string_to_array` to produce a mismatched array length with the other parameter, triggering a PostgreSQL `unnest` error that crashes the supply endpoint and effectively freezes fund release decisions?
